lines.text: 54-year-old woman with newly diagnosed de novo metastatic invasive ductal carcinoma of the breast (AJCC 8th ed, cT2N1M1).
Core biopsy (liver): HER2 IHC 3+ positive, ISH amplified (HER2/CEP17 ratio 4.2). ER 85%, PR 70% by local lab (ASCO-CAP).
NGS: PIK3CA E545K detected; no germline BRCA1/2 pathogenic variant on panel.
No prior chemotherapy or HER2-targeted therapy for metastatic disease. Adjuvant anastrozole 2020–2022 for early-stage disease (>6 months before metastatic diagnosis).
ECOG performance status 1; ambulatory and capable of self-care.
PET-CT 2026-05-20: 2.8 cm liver segment VI lesion SUVmax 8.4 (measurable per RECIST 1.1); no brain metastases on MRI.
Brain MRI 2026-05-22: no intracranial metastases; neurologically asymptomatic.
2026-06-09: ANC 2.8, Hgb 12.1, Plt 198k, total bili 0.6, Cr 0.8, AST 22, ALT 19 — adequate bone marrow and organ function.
No history of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis. No prior trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) exposure.
Echocardiogram 2026-06-07: LVEF 62%; suitable for HER2-directed therapy per cardiology.
Patient able to understand and willing to sign informed consent for clinical trial screening.
Oncology assessment: first-line metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer candidate; life expectancy estimated >6 months.
